violations.text: Various food items were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Dates were added to food items during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2026

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Sanitizing solution was adjusted during the time of inspection and was observed at 100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2026
The floor tiles in the walk-in cooler were observed to heavily cracked and are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/11/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (deli slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Deli slicer was disassembled and cleaned during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2025

Various sliced cheeses and food items were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. PIC labeled food items during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2025

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of various dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Bowls were removed from containers during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/07/2025
